summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates environment variables and filesystem secrets to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvesting routine. It enumerates every entry of process.env and runs `find` across /root, /home, /etc, /var, /tmp, /app, /opt, /srv, /workspace, and /usr/src/app for files matching `*flag*`, `*secret*`, and `*htb*`, reading any matches. The harvested content is HTTP-PUT to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-472839 (with localhost fallbacks). Although the script self-describes as a CTF payload, the mechanism scrapes arbitrary installer-side secrets (cloud credentials, API tokens, files named *secret*) and ships them to attacker-controlled infrastructure. A secondary behavior writes the harvested content into web-server document roots (/usr/share/nginx/html, /var/www/html, /app/public, /srv/http, etc.), which can publicly expose the captured secrets via the host's HTTP server and tamper with served files. A `shouldReport()` gate restricts execution to Linux hosts whose cwd matches sandbox-style paths, which is a sandbox-detection / targeting filter, not a safety control.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
